review_scope: Full 9-dimension review (independent linter run, source path verification, narrative-implementation cross-check)

source_grounding.rationale: Every Cell 2-7 has dense vllm file:line references. The Cell 4 config path fix (vllm/config/scheduler.py:L56-L63, previously vllm/v1/config/scheduler.py:L70-L84) is correctly applied and independently verified against the source. All referenced source files (vllm/v1/core/sched/scheduler.py, vllm/v1/request.py, vllm/config/scheduler.py) exist in the source submodule. Reference density: Cell 2 (4 refs), Cell 3 (5 refs), Cell 4 (7 refs), Cell 5 (8 refs), Cell 6 (10 refs), Cell 7 (6 refs). No 3+ consecutive paragraphs without source trails.

theory_depth.rationale: Strong formal derivations: (1) bubble ratio lower bound R_static ≥ 1 - ΣP_i/(N·max P_i) with equality condition analysis; (2) CB step count lower bound T_CB ≥ ceil(S/B) with optimality argument; (3) utilization comparison η_static vs η_CB with structural vs incidental bubble analysis. The Running-First design is justified with 3 theoretical reasons (protecting KV Cache investment, reducing preemption churn, minimizing unfairness). Minor limitation: the bubble ratio formula uses only prompt tokens P_i, not output tokens O_i — the derivation is valid as a lower bound but the narrative could clarify that decode-phase bubbles are additive and make the problem even worse.
formulas_verified: T_static = max P_i + max O_i — correct for the static model
η_static = Σ(P_i+O_i) / ((max P_i+max O_i)×N) — arithmetically correct
R_static formula — mathematically valid lower bound
R_CB ≤ (S mod B) / (ceil(S/B)×B) — correct upper bound for CB's residual waste

code_walkthrough.rationale: Cell 5 provides a comprehensive 81-line walkthrough of schedule() (3 phases, ~100 lines of code) with 8 specific REFERENCE annotations to vllm source. Each key decision point is explained: (1) Line 355 budget constraint with min(), (2) Lines 364-376 preempt retry loop with while-True, (3) Line 375 self-preempt guard, (4) Line 392 chunked prefill via budget cap, (5) Lines 406-409 Phase 2 no-preempt design decision. Cell 6 covers all 4 core data structures (Request, RequestStatus, KVCache, Preempt) with implementation snippets and source diffs. The walkthrough explicitly flags vLLM differences (encoder budget, spec tokens, prefix cache hits, remote KV) rather than pretending they don't exist.

numerical_example.rationale: Two concrete scenarios with full request configurations: Scenario A (2 short + 1 long, 3 requests) and Scenario B (late-arrival Delta at step 6, 4 requests). Both include step-by-step snapshots (steps 1, 5, 10) with ASCII progress bars showing per-request output progress. Demo output snippets with exact numbers (72.9% utilization, 13 idle slots, 16 static steps). The 'Completion order' output proves Delta (joined step 6, finished 3rd of 4) — demonstrating that completion order depends on length, not arrival time. Minor: could benefit from a structured table showing intermediate scheduler state (num_computed, num_new, token_budget_remaining, KV_free_blocks) across steps rather than relying on demo print output.
scenarios: Scenario A: 3 requests (A:8, B:8, C:16 output tokens) — traces steps 1-17, shows bubble elimination
Scenario B: 4 requests with Delta arriving mid-execution — proves seamless integration

readability.rationale: The cafeteria analogy (食堂拼桌) is introduced in Cell 2 and consistently maintained through the entire chapter: token budget = table seats, running = eating, waiting = in line, preempt = asked to leave. The mapping table (Cell 2) makes the analogy explicit. Structure flows logically: concept → problem → theory → code → data structures → numerical trace → source map → verification → summary. Chinese text is clear and accessible for a zero-basis reader. The chapter-opening quote from scheduler.py:L353-L362 establishes the central thesis immediately. The 'Key' notation for ASCII diagrams is helpful.

formula_correctness.rationale: Linter: ALL checks passed (run independently). All formulas use correct LaTeX conventions: \mathrm{} not \text{}, no \boxed{}, no \tag*{}, \frac only in $$ blocks. Semantic verification: all formulas are arithmetically self-consistent. The utilization formula η_static = Σ(P_i+O_i)/((max P_i+max O_i)×N) correctly reflects total useful work / total capacity. The CB step count bound ceil(S/B) correctly models token-level parallelism. Minor: the bubble ratio derivation R_static ≥ 1 - ΣP_i/(N·max P_i) only accounts for prefill-phase bubbles (uses P_i only, not O_i). For decode-heavy workloads, an additive term for O_i variation would strengthen the argument. The numerical example (35/48 ≈ 72.9%) is verified correct: (9+9+17)/(16×3) = 35/48.

diagram_quality.rationale: Four diagrams with Python generation scripts in diagrams/: bubble_comparison (SVG+PNG, 221KB), budget_allocation (SVG+PNG, 199KB), cafeteria_analogy (SVG+PNG, 199KB), state_machine (SVG+PNG, 228KB). All have both SVG source and rendered PNG. The state machine diagram directly supports Cell 6's request lifecycle explanation. The bubble comparison visualizes the static vs continuous contrast. The diagrams are high-resolution PNGs suitable for publication. Minor: the narrative does not explicitly reference the diagram files within the text — embedding diagram references (e.g., 'See Figure X') would strengthen the integration.

completeness.rationale: All required cells present: Cell 2 (opening analogy, ~42 lines), Cell 3 (problem demonstration with ASCII bubble diagram, ~76 lines), Cell 4 (formal theory with historical context, ~122 lines), Cell 5 (code walkthrough, ~120 lines), Cell 6 (data structures, ~112 lines), Cell 7 (numerical examples, ~97 lines), Cell 9 (source mapping table, ~30 lines), Cell 10 (verification with test results, ~60 lines), Cell 11 (summary + next chapter preview, ~35 lines). Each cell has a dedicated Source Trail subsection. Cell 10 includes test count breakdown (58 tests, 9 categories), linter invocation commands, KV block conservation invariant, and static-vs-continuous comparison assertion.

consistency.rationale: Implementation-narrative cross-check: (1) Phase 1 running-first matches scheduler.py:L343-L382, (2) Phase 2 waiting admission with break-on-OOM matches L384-L409, (3) Phase 3 auto-regressive simulation matches L411-L431, (4) _pick_preemption_victim using max(priority, request_id) matches L296-L306, (5) _preempt_request not resetting num_computed_tokens matches L308-L325 (with explicit justification in docstring and narrative), (6) All 58 tests pass (independently verified: 58/58, 0.34s). Minor discrepancy: Cell 3 line 81 says '8 steps × 2 idle slots = 16 wasted slot-steps' (ASCII mental math) while the simulator output at line 122 reports 'Idle slots wasted: 13'. The 16 vs 13 discrepancy stems from the ASCII diagram's simplified 8×2 calculation vs the simulator's precise step-by-step counting. The simulator's 13 is the authoritative number (35 useful / 48 total = 72.9%, 48-35=13 idle). The narrative uses the simulator output in all subsequent references (Cells 3, 7, 10), so the ASCII annotation is the only outlier. Not blocking — the narrative consistently uses the simulator's 13 in all quantitative claims.

missing_mathematical_proof.detail: Cell 4 contains formal proofs for bubble ratio, step count bounds, and utilization comparison.

missing_code_walkthrough.detail: Cell 5 provides detailed line-by-line walkthrough of schedule() with 8 REFERENCE annotations.

text_in_formulas.detail: Linter confirmed: zero instances of \text{} in LaTeX blocks.

boxed_in_formulas.detail: Linter confirmed: zero instances of \boxed{} in LaTeX blocks.

zero_source_references.detail: All Cells 2-7 have dense vllm file:line references. No 3+ consecutive paragraphs without source trails.

duplicate_ids.detail: Cell IDs are unique (2,3,4,5,6,7,9,10,11). No chapter ID collision.

suggestions.description: ASCII diagram annotation says '8 steps × 2 idle slots = 16 wasted slot-steps' but the simulator reports '13 idle slots'. The ASCII mental math (8×2) oversimplifies. Suggest changing the annotation to reference the simulator's exact count (13) or explicitly noting that the ASCII diagram is a simplified approximation. The narrative already uses 13 in all quantitative sections — this is just about the diagram annotation.

The bubble ratio derivation R_static ≥ 1 - ΣP_i/(N·max P_i) uses only prompt tokens P_i. Adding a note that decode-phase length variation produces additive bubbles (using O_i analogously) would strengthen the theoretical argument and make the proof complete for the full prefill+decode lifecycle.

Consider adding a structured table showing intermediate scheduler variables (num_computed, num_new, token_budget_remaining, KV_free_blocks, running_list_length) for steps 1, 5, 9, 10, 17 of Scenario A. This would complement the demo output snapshots with programmatic state tracking.
The 4 diagrams in diagrams/ are not explicitly referenced in the narrative text. Adding inline references (e.g., '如图 X 所示...') in Cells 2, 3, 4, and 6 would strengthen the visual-textual integration.

linter_results.formula: PASS — All formula checks passed (run independently, not trusting writer's self-check). Zero violations of \text{}, \boxed{}, \tag*{}, or inline \frac rules.
linter_results.source_grounding: PASS — All grounding checks passed (run independently). Every Cell has sufficient vllm source file:line references. Source Mapping Table has 12 rows (≥5 required). impl-notes.md lists 11 source files (≥3 required).
